revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]]{{ill|Magaibutsu|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:磨崖仏]] carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:阿武隈川]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]], a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:福島空港]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:阿弥陀如来]] or {{ill|Vairocana|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:大日如来]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:空海]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:横穴墓]], believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:古墳時代]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:市指定史跡]] of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:須賀川牡丹園]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:乙字ケ滝]] * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:福島空港公園]] Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:須賀川インターチェンジ]], {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:東北自動車道]] * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:川東駅]], {{ill|Suigun Line|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:水郡線]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:JR東日本]]
== References == {{Reflist]]
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs

=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]]{{ill|Magaibutsu|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:磨崖仏]] carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:阿武隈川]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]], a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:福島空港]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:阿弥陀如来]] or {{ill|Vairocana|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:大日如来]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:空海]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:横穴墓]], believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:古墳時代]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:市指定史跡]] of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:須賀川牡丹園]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:乙字ケ滝]] * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:福島空港公園]] Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:須賀川インターチェンジ]], {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:東北自動車道]] * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:川東駅]], {{ill|Suigun Line|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:水郡線]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:JR東日本]]
== References == {{Reflist]]
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]]{{ill|Magaibutsu|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:磨崖仏]] carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:阿武隈川]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]], a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:福島空港]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:阿弥陀如来]] or {{ill|Vairocana|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:大日如来]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:空海]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:横穴墓]], believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:古墳時代]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:市指定史跡]] of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:須賀川牡丹園]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:乙字ケ滝]] * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:福島空港公園]] Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:須賀川インターチェンジ]], {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:東北自動車道]] * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:川東駅]], {{ill|Suigun Line|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:水郡線]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:JR東日本]]
== References == {{Reflist]]
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]]{{ill|Magaibutsu|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:磨崖仏]] carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:阿武隈川]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]], a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:福島空港]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:阿弥陀如来]] or {{ill|Vairocana|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大日如来]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:空海]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:横穴墓]], believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:古墳時代]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:市指定史跡]] of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:須賀川牡丹園]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:乙字ケ滝]] * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:福島空港公園]] Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:須賀川インターチェンジ]], {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:東北自動車道]] * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:川東駅]], {{ill|Suigun Line|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:水郡線]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:JR東日本]]

=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:十五郎穴横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en]][[ja:ひたちなか市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karanogosho Cave Tomb|en]][[ja:唐御所横穴]] ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en]][[ja:那珂川町]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|en]][[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya|en]][[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|en]][[ja:吉見百穴]] ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|en]][[ja:吉見町]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:十郎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|en]][[ja:鳩山町]], Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en]][[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en]][[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en]][[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en]][[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en]][[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en]][[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en]][[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en]][[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群|en]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:七石山横穴墓群|en]][[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:柏谷横穴群|en]][[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en]][[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en]][[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en]][[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:法皇山横穴古墳|en]][[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en]][[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en]][[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en]][[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en]][[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en]][[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en]][[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en]][[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en]][[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en]][[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en]][[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en]][[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en]][[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en]][[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en]][[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en]][[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en]][[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en]][[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en]][[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en]][[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:New pages]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]]{{ill|Magaibutsu|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:磨崖仏]] carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:阿武隈川]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]], a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島空港]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:阿弥陀如来]] or {{ill|Vairocana|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:大日如来]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:空海]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:横穴墓]], believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:古墳時代]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:市指定史跡]] of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:須賀川牡丹園]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:乙字ケ滝]] * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島空港公園]] Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:須賀川インターチェンジ]], {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:東北自動車道]] * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:川東駅]], {{ill|Suigun Line|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:水郡線]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:JR東日本]]
== References == {{Reflist]]

National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en]][[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en]][[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en]][[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en]][[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群|en]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:七石山横穴墓群|en]][[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群|en]][[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en]][[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en]][[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en]][[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:法皇山横穴古墳|en]][[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en]][[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en]][[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en]][[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en]][[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en]][[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en]][[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en]][[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en]][[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en]][[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en]][[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en]][[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en]][[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en]][[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en]][[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en]][[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en]][[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en]][[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en]][[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en]][[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴墓]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]]{{ill|Magaibutsu|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:磨崖仏]] carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:阿武隈川]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]], a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島空港]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:阿弥陀如来]] or {{ill|Vairocana|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:大日如来]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:空海]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:横穴墓]], believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:古墳時代]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:市指定史跡]] of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:須賀川牡丹園]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:乙字ケ滝]] * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島空港公園]] Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:須賀川インターチェンジ]], {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:東北自動車道]] * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:川東駅]], {{ill|Suigun Line|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:水郡線]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:JR東日本]]
== References == {{Reflist]]
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:十五郎穴横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en]][[ja:ひたちなか市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karanogosho Cave Tomb|en]][[ja:唐御所横穴]] ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en]][[ja:那珂川町]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:十郎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|en]][[ja:鳩山町]], Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en]][[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en]][[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en]][[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en]][[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en]][[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en]][[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en]][[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en]][[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群|en]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:七石山横穴墓群|en]][[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群|en]][[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en]][[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en]][[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en]][[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:法皇山横穴古墳|en]][[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en]][[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en]][[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en]][[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en]][[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en]][[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en]][[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en]][[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en]][[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en]][[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en]][[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en]][[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en]][[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en]][[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en]][[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en]][[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en]][[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en]][[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en]][[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en]][[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Yokohama Tomb" to "Cave Tomb"
revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]]{{ill|Magaibutsu|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:磨崖仏]] carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:阿武隈川]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]], a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島空港]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:阿弥陀如来]] or {{ill|Vairocana|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:大日如来]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:空海]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:横穴墓]], believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:古墳時代]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:市指定史跡]] of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:須賀川牡丹園]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:乙字ケ滝]] * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島空港公園]] Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:須賀川インターチェンジ]], {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:東北自動車道]] * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:川東駅]], {{ill|Suigun Line|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:水郡線]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:JR東日本]]
== References == {{Reflist]]
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en]][[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en]][[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en]][[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en]][[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en]][[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en]][[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en]][[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en]][[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群|en]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:七石山横穴墓群|en]][[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群|en]][[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en]][[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en]][[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en]][[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:法皇山横穴古墳|en]][[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en]][[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en]][[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en]][[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en]][[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en]][[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en]][[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en]][[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en]][[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en]][[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en]][[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en]][[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en]][[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en]][[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en]][[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en]][[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en]][[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en]][[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en]][[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en]][[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en]][[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Yokoguchi" to "Cave Tomb"
revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]]{{ill|Magaibutsu|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:磨崖仏]] carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:阿武隈川]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]], a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島空港]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:阿弥陀如来]] or {{ill|Vairocana|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:大日如来]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:空海]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:横穴墓]], believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:古墳時代]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:市指定史跡]] of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:須賀川牡丹園]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:乙字ケ滝]] * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島空港公園]] Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:須賀川インターチェンジ]], {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:東北自動車道]] * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:川東駅]], {{ill|Suigun Line|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:水郡線]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:JR東日本]]
== References == {{Reflist]]
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en]][[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en]][[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en]][[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en]][[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en]][[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en]][[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群|en]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:七石山横穴墓群|en]][[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群|en]][[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en]][[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en]][[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en]][[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:法皇山横穴古墳|en]][[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en]][[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en]][[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en]][[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en]][[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en]][[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en]][[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en]][[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en]][[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en]][[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en]][[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en]][[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en]][[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en]][[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en]][[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en]][[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en]][[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en]][[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en]][[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en]][[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Eura Cave Tomb Cluster" to "E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster"
revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]]{{ill|Magaibutsu|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:磨崖仏]] carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:阿武隈川]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]], a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島空港]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:阿弥陀如来]] or {{ill|Vairocana|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:大日如来]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:空海]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:横穴墓]], believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:古墳時代]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:市指定史跡]] of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:須賀川牡丹園]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:乙字ケ滝]] * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:福島空港公園]] Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:須賀川インターチェンジ]], {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:東北自動車道]] * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:川東駅]], {{ill|Suigun Line|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:水郡線]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:JR東日本]]
== References == {{Reflist]]
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en]][[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en]][[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en]][[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en]][[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群|en]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:七石山横穴墓群|en]][[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en]][[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群|en]][[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en]][[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en]][[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en]][[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||en]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Magaibutsu-qq]] [[ja:法皇山横穴古墳|en]][[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en]][[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en]][[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en]][[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en]][[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en]][[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en]][[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en]][[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en]][[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en]][[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en]][[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en]][[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en]][[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en]][[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en]][[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en]][[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en]][[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en]][[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en]][[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en]][[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en]][[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en]][[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
